rationale: The US-Iran exchange of strikes and Hormuz closure continues for a second day with no qualitative escalation beyond what was already known — energy price risk for Poland persists but this is now background; no new events this cycle exceed the threshold for a Polish user.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue, Hormuz situation ongoing
top_events.summary: US and Iran exchanged strikes across the Middle East for a second consecutive day, with questions raised about whether Hormuz was truly 'newly' closed or already restricted. No qualitative escalation beyond the established pattern — energy price risk for Poland (fuel, heating) remains but is unchanged from the previous cycle.

top_events.title: US inflation at 3-year high, Trump indifferent
top_events.summary: Trump stated 'I love the inflation' as US prices rose at their fastest rate in three years. Persistent US inflation and unpredictable trade policy indirectly affect Polish export conditions, EU-US trade relations, and global financial stability — a mild but real background risk.

top_events.title: UK Defence Secretary Healey resigns over spending
top_events.summary: Britain's Defence Secretary Healey has quit over defence spending disputes. This is a continued story with indirect relevance to NATO cohesion — no new development this cycle, fading further into background.
